welcome_p1: This test measures your working memory — the ability to hold and update information in your mind.
welcome_p2: You will see a series of letters, one at a time. Your task is to decide whether the current letter matches the one shown N steps earlier in the sequence.
welcome_p3: The test is set to {{nLevel}}-back. You can adjust the difficulty in the settings.
tutorial_step1_text: Letters will appear one at a time in the center of the screen. Each letter is shown briefly, then a blank screen appears before the next letter.
tutorial_step2_text: For each letter, decide if it matches the letter shown {{nLevel}} positions earlier in the sequence. For example, in 2-back: if the sequence is B, G, B — the third letter B matches the letter 2 steps back.
tutorial_step3_text: Press F if the current letter matches the one N steps back (target). Press J if it does not match (non-target). You can also click the buttons on screen.
tutorial_step4_text: You have a limited time to respond to each letter. Both your accuracy and reaction time are measured. Try to be as fast and accurate as possible.
demo_intro: Watch a 2-back example to understand how the test works.
demo_step1: The test begins. A fixation cross (+) appears. Get ready.
demo_step2: First letter: B — No comparison possible yet (no letter 2 steps back). No response needed.
demo_step3: Second letter: G — Still no letter 2 steps back. No response needed.
demo_step4: Third letter: B — This matches the letter from 2 steps back (B). Press F for match!
demo_step5: Fourth letter: T — The letter 2 steps back was G. T does not match G. Press J for no match.
demo_step6: You understand how it works. Ready to practice?
practice_complete_text1: Well done! You have completed the practice round.
practice_complete_text2: The real test uses {{nLevel}}-back. Remember: press F for match, J for no match.
countdown_instruction: Press F for match, J for no match
bottom_instruction: Press F if the letter matches the one N steps back, J if it does not
settings_n_level_desc: How many steps back to compare (1 = easiest, 3 = hardest)
settings_trials_desc: Number of scorable trials (excluding initial non-scorable trials)
settings_target_pct_desc: Proportion of trials that are targets (matches)
settings_stimulus_duration_desc: How long each letter is displayed on screen
settings_response_window_desc: Maximum time allowed to respond to each letter
footer_description: Measure working memory with the N-Back task.
